title: Xendor
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Youmay|Xendor, the leader of the [[Legions of Lettow]]|the phrase "[[Minions of Xendor]]"}}

{{Quote|Even now, you refuse to understand. There are as many truths to the Force as there are hearts within which the Force manifests itself. The existence of the triad has no more bearing on the reality of the Force than the Ashla and the Bogan, or anything I tell you, or anything you tell others. Any philosophy, creed or religion that opens the heart to the Force proves itself to be true. My legions follow the dictates of such a creed. But that is only a demonstration of the application of power, Arden. It says nothing about the rightness of our beliefs, or the universality of our faith.|Xendor|<ref name="TEGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>}}
'''Xendor''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[General/Legends|general]] of the [[Legions of Lettow]], a group of [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitives]] who studied and wielded the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. A [[Kashi Mer Dynasty]] exile, he spoke out against the teachings of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and this philosophical disagreement eventually lead to open war with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. With his lover [[Arden Lyn]] at his side, he fought in the war of the [[First Great Schism]] and was eventually killed on the day of the Legions' [[Battle of Columus|defeat]] at [[Columus/Legends|Columus]].

Lyn survived, but was placed in a deep trance following a confrontation with [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Awdrysta Pina]]. As the [[New Sith Wars]] raged, rumors abounded of Xendor's return in the form of the enigmatic [[Dark Underlord]]. By the time of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], Xendor took on new life as a popular curse uttered by [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]]. During this time, Lyn was awoken from her preserving trance, and she made several attempts to resurrect Xendor. [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] killed her sometime after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Biography==
===The First Great Schism===
[[File:Arden_Lyn_and_Xendor.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Xendor with Arden Lyn, his right hand]]
{{Quote|They called themselves the Legions of Lettow, and they were led by General Xendor, a powerful Force-using humanoid whose origins remain unknown.|Tionne Solusar|<ref name="jvs" />}}
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="TEGTW" /> of the [[Kashi Mer Dynasty]], Xendor was exiled after he began to use the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], in conjunction with the use of the [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|Teräs Käsi]] martial art.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|III|341|Xendor}}</ref> Upon departing home, Xendor took with him a [[Kashi Mer talisman]].<ref name="TEGTW" /> Taking refuge with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], Xendor became a well respected [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. During his time on Ossus, Xendor became increasingly outspoken about what he saw as the Order's progressively more close-minded attitude towards other Force-traditions and the study of the Dark side. During this time, Xendor met a [[Followers of Palawa|Steel Hand of Palawa]] named [[Arden Lyn]] and began to learn about the philosophies of [[Palawa]], eventually taking her as a lover.<ref name="TEGTW" />

Xendor, able to bear his frustration no longer, approached the High Council and requested their blessings in opening a new [[Jedi academy]] far from Ossus to study the dark side. When the Council refused, Xendor quietly broke with the Order and traveled to the planet [[Lettow]] to open an [[Legionnaire Academy|academy]] which allowed the dark side to be studied. Drawing on Force traditions such as the [[Order of Dai Bendu|Dai Bendu]], the [[Followers of Palawa]], the Kashi Mer [[Guardians of the Breath|Guardians of Breath]], the [[Baran Do]], and the [[Chatos Academy]], Xendor's academy drew a trickle of interest which quickly grew into a large following. It was these acolytes which named their new group the [[Legions of Lettow]], as they believed that they would one day be forced to defend their traditions from the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]'s wrath.<ref name="TEGTW" />

Sometime during Xendor's life, he traveled to a [[Mortis/Legends|dead planet]] where the Force was worshiped as a [[The Ones/Legends|trio]] of beings. He would later recount the tale to Arden Lyn, telling her of the [[Daughter/Legends|Daughter]], the embodiment of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]], and the [[Son/Legends|Son]], the embodiment of the dark side. When Lyn asked about the [[Father (Mortis)/Legends|Father]], he cryptically replied that he might have been the Force itself. When asked what he had discovered, Xendor simply laughed and told her that there were many truths to the Force—the existence of the triad had no bearing on the truth of the Force, any more than the views of the Legions or the Jedi did.<ref name="TEGTW" />

In [[24,500 BBY]],<ref name="jvs" /> Xendor took the rank of [[General/Legends|general]] and lead the Legions of Lettow in open warfare against the Jedi Order. Known to history as the [[First Great Schism]], Xendor took the war directly to Ossus in a pre-emptive strike against the Jedi. During the [[Battle of Ossus (First Great Schism)|battle]], Xendor's forces were pushed back and forced to commit troops to several fronts as the Order pushed the Legionnaires towards the [[Core Worlds/Legends|galaxy's core]]. In an attempt to ally with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] against the Jedi Order, he accused the Jedi of attempting to subvert the Republic for their own ends. Xendor was ignored and branded as a warlord by the Republic. Campaigns were fought on [[Battle of Chandrila (First Great Schism)|Chandrila]], [[Battle of Metellos (First Great Schism)|Metellos]], [[Battle of Brentaal IV (First Great Schism)|Brentaal IV]] and [[Battle of Coruscant (First Great Schism)|Coruscant]] until the Jedi leader,<ref name="TEGTW" /> [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]]<ref name="Force">[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]]</ref> [[Awdrysta Pina]] managed to come into direct [[Battle of Columus|battle]] with Xendor on [[Columus/Legends|Columus]]. Known as the Green Blade, Master Pina and his [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] were able to separate Xendor's vanguard from the bulk of his army through the use of a [[Force-meld|strong Force meld]]. Cutting through powerful warriors such as [[Sethul Asaiage]] and [[Tun Bohoi]], Pina eventually dueled Xendor and slew him in hand-to-hand combat,<ref name="TEGTW" /> and his spirit plunged into [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]].<ref name="tep">{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}}</ref> News of this was transmitted to even Jedi [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] on far-flung [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], signifying what was thought by some to be the immediate end to the war.<ref name="jvs" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|It seems too easy to blame everything on Xendor and the dark side. Maybe we… maybe the Jedi were partly to blame, too.|[[Danzigorro Potts]]|<ref name="jvs" />}}
On the day that Xendor died, the Legions lost the [[Battle of Columus]], with the sole survivor being Jedi Padawan [[Danzigorro Potts]]. Before dying of his own mortal wounds sustained in the battle, Potts made a recording on a [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] that would become and remain famous for thousands of years after the First Great Schism. In it, the Padawan speculated on just how and why Xendor had been able to sway so many to his cause. In the wake of Columus and Xendor's [[Death/Legends|death]], Arden Lyn led the defeated Legions of Lettow.<ref name="jvs" /> In the aftermath of the schism, [[Awdrysta Pina]] hunted Lyn down, forcing her to [[Duel on Irkalla|destroy]] her assailant with Xendor's [[Kashi Mer talisman]]. Prior to perishing, Pina used the technique of [[Morichro/Legends|''morichro'']] to stop Lyn's heart, and as a result, the Follower of Palawa fell into a deep and long trance that preserved her.<ref name="TEGTW" />

[[File:ArdenLyn-NEGTC.png|thumb|right|Arden Lyn]]
The First Great Schism would prove to be the first of several conflicts between the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]] and dark sides of the Force. Over the centuries, various wars stemming from the dispute between the two schools of thought would be waged, at a tremendous loss of life.<ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> During the [[New Sith Wars]] instigated by [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Darth Ruin]] in [[2000 BBY/Legends|2000 BBY]], an enigmatic figure known only as the [[Dark Underlord]] rose to power. Speculation abounded as to his origins, with one theory suggesting that he was in fact the returned spirit of Xendor. The Dark Underlord was eventually destroyed by the Jedi Master [[Murrtaggh]].<ref name="end">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref>

In [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]],<ref name="TEGTW" /> [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Imperial Inquisitors]]—[[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] in the service of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], awoke Arden Lyn. Instead of being annihilated, Lyn was converted to Palpatine's cause and served him as one of the [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hands]]. In turn, he promised her that he would summon Xendor's spirit back from Chaos. Lyn soon realized that Palpatine did not intend to make good on his promise and participated in an attempted coup against him led by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. When that failed, she scoured [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for the Kashi Mer talisman that she had possessed in her earlier life, hoping to regain the influence she had enjoyed during the Schism. She also intended to resurrect Xendor herself with the power of the talisman.<ref name="tep" /> Palpatine killed Lyn sometime after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="jvs" />

During Palpatine's reign, Xendor's name would be frequently taken in vain by [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] such as [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. The phrase "[[Minions of Xendor]]" became a popular curse during this time, particularly with Solo.<ref name="tps">''[[The Paradise Snare]]''</ref><ref name="hg">''[[The Hutt Gambit]]''</ref><ref name="rd">''[[Rebel Dawn]]''</ref> In [[36 ABY]], Xendor found his way into the writings of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] historian [[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]],<ref name="nec" /> and [[40 ABY/Legends|four years later]], into the writings of [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]]. The contents of Danzigorro Potts' data card, known popularly as the [[Columus Data Card]], were collected in Solusar's writings. The card had been the inspiration for at least three operas on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], but had been decried by historian [[Ingo Wavlud]] as mere Jedi propaganda. Even by this stage, Xendor's origins remained a mystery, at least to the [[New Jedi Order]].<ref name="jvs" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|He was respected in the order, but vocal in his disapproval of its increasingly exclusionary ways.|Arden Lyn|<ref name="TEGTW" />}}
A powerful<ref name="jvs" /> and, according to Sith sources, courageous<ref name="hots">{{InsiderCite|88|Heritage of the Sith}}</ref> [[Human/Legends|Human]] man,<ref name="TEGTW" /> Xendor posed the first true threat to the Jedi Order. By highlighting and emphasizing the routine and, in the view of his followers, stifling nature of the Jedi way, his libertine view was able to sway legions of Jedi to his cause—even experienced Jedi Masters.<ref name="jvs" /> Xendor had dark skin and dark hair.<ref name="TEGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Mention of Lyn's lover 'Xendor and his minions' was a bit of tongue-in-cheek fun. Prior to this, Xendor was little more than an unspecified noun making up part of one of Lando's preferred exclamations…|Abel G. Peña|<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|abelgpena|84|The Emperor's Pawns Endnotes, Part 4: Mistress of Teräs Käsi}}</ref>}}
Xendor was first mentioned in [[A. C. Crispin]]'s ''[[The Paradise Snare]]'', where the name was used repeatedly in various curses uttered by characters. The name was mentioned in much the same capacity in the two other books in that series, ''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'' and ''[[Rebel Dawn]]''. The character of Xendor was greatly embellished upon by [[Abel G. Peña]] in his article ''[[The Emperor's Pawns]]'', which was published in [[Star Wars Gamer 5|''Star Wars Gamer'' 5]]. Further, fleeting mentions were made by [[Daniel Wallace]] in ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', as well as by [[Drew Karpyshyn]] in the article ''[[Heritage of the Sith]]'', published in [[Star Wars Insider 88|''Star Wars Insider'' 88]]. Peña once again used the character briefly in ''[[Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties]]'', and [[Ryder Windham]] would, in [[2007]], expand upon the character once more in [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]. The [[2012]] reference book ''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' provided a picture of Xendor.

===Continuity===
In ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'', Arden Lyn states that the Jedi and the Legionnaires did not wield lightsabers at the time,<ref name="TEGTW" /> but ''Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side'' depicts Xendor and his followers wielding red-bladed lightsabers.<ref name="Book">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>
